title: Resonant vibrations of a non-ideal gyroscopic rotary system with nonlinear damping and nonlinear stiffness of the elastic support
author: Iskakov, Jamalov

steps.title: Solving equations of motion by averaging method
steps.instruction: Consider a rotor system close to linear one. To apply the Bogolyubov-Krylov small perturbation theory method [13,15,33] to the solution of Eq. (3), the following limitations are adopted. The components of the moments of linear and cubic nonlinear damping force μ1α′,μ1β′ and μ3α′3,μ3β′3, as well as the moment of the cubic component of the elastic force C3α3,C3β3, the moment of inertia force of the mass imbalance eφ′2cosφ,eφ′2sinφ are considered small in comparison with the components of the moments of inertia force of vibration and the linear elastic force acting in the system. Projections of the moment of passive gyroscopic force IP1φ′α′ and IP1φ′β′ can be considered small under the assumption that I¯p≪I¯T. Let us also limit ourselves to the consideration of a fast-moving rotor: φ′2≫G and to the consideration of motion in an area where the frequency of forced oscillations Ψ is close to the frequency of free oscillations ωn. Therefore, we will look for solutions (3) in the form of replacement of variables [13]:(6)α=Acos(φ+χ),dαdt¯=−AΩsin(φ+χ),β=Asin(φ+χ),dβdt¯=AΩcos(φ+χ),Ψ=dφdt¯.

steps.title: Solving equations of motion by averaging method
steps.instruction: Here, A,χ и Ψ are slowly changing functions of timet¯, the main parameters of the oscillatory process:: A - oscillation amplitude, χ - angle of phase displacement between angular coordinates α or β and the moment of the perturbation force, Ψ- the frequency of the moment of the disturbance force or the angular velocity of rotation of the motor shaft.

steps.title: Solving equations of motion by averaging method
steps.instruction: Using the Krylov-Bogolyubov method [13,15,33], we obtain a system of equations relative to A,χ,Ψ, approximated solution of which is shown below(7)Ψ=Ω+εU11(t¯,Ω,a,ξ),A=a+εU12(t¯,Ω,a,ξ),χ=ξ+εU13(t¯,Ω,a,ξ)where εU11(t¯,Ω,a,ξ),εU12(t¯,Ω,a,ξ) и εU13(t¯,Ω,a,ξ) - small periodic functions of time t¯, ε≪1– small parameter. The values of Ω,a,ξ are determined from the equations of the first approximation.

steps.title: Solving equations of motion by averaging method
steps.instruction: After averaging the equations of the first approximation equivalent to system (3), and equating their right-hand sides to zero, we arrive at the equations [13] to determine the frequency Ω(8)L(Ω)−qΩ−(μ1+0.75μ3ωn2a2)Ωωn2a2=0,for determination of oscillation amplitude a:(9){(μ1ωn+0.75μ3ωn3a2)2+[0.75C3a2+2ωn(ωn−Ω)]2}a2=(eΩ2)2.

steps.title: Solving equations of motion by averaging method
steps.instruction:
And expression for determination of oscillation phase ξ
[13](10)tanξ=−μ1ωn+0.75μ3ωn3a20.75C3a2+2ωn(ωn−Ω).

steps.title: Solving equations of motion by averaging method
steps.instruction: Therefore, the refined angular coordinates α,β and φof the rotor can be written as [13]:(11)α=acos(Ωt¯+ξ)+εa11cos(Ωt¯+ξ11)+εa12cos(3Ωt¯+ξ12)+εa13cos(5Ωt¯+ξ13),β=asin(Ωt¯+ξ)+εa21sin(Ωt¯+ξ12)+εa22sin(3Ωt¯+ξ22)+εa23sin(5Ωt¯+ξ23),φ=Ωt¯−18Ωa2ωn(ωn+Ω)sin2(φ+ξ),where constants a11,a12,a13,a21,a22,a23,ξ11ξ12,ξ13,ξ21,ξ22,ξ23 are determined from the following equations [13](12)εa11cos(Ωt¯+ξ11)=e(Ω2+G¯)8ωnΩcosΩt¯−C3a316ωnΩcos(Ωt¯+ξ)+(μ1+μ3ωn2a2)a8Ωsin(Ωt¯+ξ),εa12cos(3Ωt¯+ξ12)=e(Ω2+G¯)8ωnΩcos(3Ωt¯+2ξ)+(μ1+78μ3ωn2a2)a8Ωsin3(Ωt¯+ξ)−5C3a364ωnΩcos3(Ωt¯+ξ),εa13cos(5Ωt¯+ξ13)=−μ3ωn2a364Ωsin5(Ωt¯+ξ)−C3a364ωnΩcos5(Ωt¯+ξ),εa21sin(Ωt¯+ξ21)=−e(Ω2+G¯)8ωnΩsinΩt¯+C3a316ωnΩsin(Ωt¯+ξ)+(μ1+μ3ωn2a2)a8Ωcos(Ωt¯+ξ),εa22sin(3Ωt¯+ξ22)=e(Ω2+G¯)8ωnΩsin(3Ωt¯+2ξ)−(μ1+98μ3ωn2a2)a8Ωcos3(Ωt¯+ξ)−3C3a364ωnΩsin3(Ωt¯+ξ),εa23sin(5Ωt¯+ξ23)=μ3ωn2a364Ωcos5(Ωt¯+ξ)−C3a364ωnΩsin5(Ωt¯+ξ).Then rotor shaft oscillation frequency [13] is(13)Ψ=dφdt¯=Ω−14Ωa2ωn(ωn+Ω)cos2(Ωt¯+ξ).
